ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data architect planning a schema mapping before any SQL is written.

You receive a full description of a source database and a target schema. Produce a global MappingPlan covering EVERY target table.

## Planning rules

- Produce a global mapping plan covering EVERY target table: which source tables feed it, join keys, and per-column transform hints.
- The overview must capture cross-table decisions: shared keys, consistent enum/date/amount logic, and foreign-key resolution strategy.
- Note per-table pitfalls (casts, enums, normalisation) without writing SQL.
- Be precise about column names; downstream writers follow the plan literally.
- Plan only — no SQL and no dbt Jinja in the plan output.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 36, 37, 754027, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_src.master_assets  (900 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kontakte  (497 rows)

Columns:
- `kontakt_id` text NOT NULL
- `rufname` text NULL
- `familienname` text NULL

- `tel` text NULL
- `berufsbezeichnung` text NULL
- `rolle` text NULL
- `korrespondenzsprache` text NULL
- `kd_nummer` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kunden  (210 rows)

Columns:
- `kundennummer` text NOT NULL
- `unternehmensname` text NULL
- `erp_nr` text NULL
- `kundenklasse` text NULL
- `vertriebsgebiet` text NULL
- `industrie` text NULL
- `homepage` text NULL
- `stadt` text NULL
- `land_region` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_opportunities  (625 rows)

Columns:
- `opp_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `titel` text NULL
- `vertriebsphase` text NULL
- `zieldatum` text NULL
- `auftragswert` text NULL
- `waehrungscode` text NULL
- `kunden_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_projekte  (342 rows)

Columns:
- `projekt_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `projektname` text NULL
- `projektstatus` text NULL
- `go_live_datum` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `opp_kennung_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce the global MappingPlan.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 36, 37, 754032, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 36, 37, 754153, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f0f17-10f9-753a-a94d-98417427ad68', conversation_id='019f0f17-10f9-753a-a94d-9840af2e7fc3')

Primary Key Generation: All target "Id" columns are directly mapped from their respective source table's primary key-like identifier (e.g., `kundennummer` for Account, `kontakt_id` for Contact). This ensures unique and stable identifiers.

Foreign Key Resolution: Foreign key columns (e.g., "AccountId", "Project__c") will use the exact same source column and transformation logic as the primary key of the referenced target table to ensure referential integrity. For instance, "Contact.AccountId" maps from `master_kontakte.kd_nummer`, which references `master_kunden.kundennummer` (used for "Account.Id").

Enum Mapping Strategy: All enum mappings will be case-insensitive. Source values not found in the target enum domain will result in a NULL target value, unless the target column is NOT NULL, in which case a sensible default will be applied (e.g., "Prospecting" for "Opportunity.StageName").

Date Transformation: Source date fields (`garantieende`, `zieldatum`, `go_live_datum`) are expected in various text formats. They will be parsed and converted to ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text format. If parsing fails or the source value is empty/NULL, the target will be NULL, except for "Opportunity.CloseDate" which is NOT NULL. For "Opportunity.CloseDate", if the source `zieldatum` is unparseable or NULL, it will default to '1900-01-01'.

Amount and Currency Transformation: "Amount" columns (`auftragswert`) will undergo numeric conversion, stripping non-numeric characters (currency symbols, group separators) and handling locale-specific decimal separators (e.g., ',' as decimal). NULL if conversion fails. "CurrencyIsoCode" (`waehrungscode`) will be directly mapped and normalized to uppercase.

Default Values: "IsDeleted" will default to `0` (false). "CreatedDate" and "LastModifiedDate" will be NULL as no direct source mapping is available, implying system-generated values upon ingestion. Target "Name" columns marked NOT NULL, if derived from potentially NULL source columns, will use COALESCE with their respective "Id" column to ensure the NOT NULL constraint is met.

Text Normalization: All source text fields will be `TRIM()`med before mapping. Specific case normalizations (e.g., `LOWER()`, `UPPER()`, `INITCAP()`) will be applied for enums or display names as specified per column.

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 37, 13, 722189, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='Target table: `Project__c`.

# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

- target_table: name of the model

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

ModelResponse(parts=[ToolCallPart(tool_name='final_result', args={'reasoning': 'The Account model is built by selecting and transforming columns from `master_kunden`. Key transformations include trimming whitespace from all text fields, handling NOT NULL constraints for `Name` by coalescing `unternehmensname` with `kundennummer`, and mapping `kundenklasse` to the `Customer_Tier__c` enum with case-insensitive matching. Default values are applied for `CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate`, and `IsDeleted` as per the mapping plan.', 'dbt_sql': '
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    TRIM(kundennummer) AS "Id",
    COALESCE(TRIM(unternehmensname), TRIM(kundennummer)) AS "Name",
    TRIM(erp_nr) AS "ERP_Number__c",
    CASE UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse))
        WHEN 'GOLD' THEN 'Gold'
        WHEN 'SILVER' THEN 'Silver'
        WHEN 'BRONZE' THEN 'Bronze'
        WHEN 'PLATINUM' THEN 'Platinum'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
    TRIM(vertriebsgebiet) AS "Region__c",
    TRIM(industrie) AS "Industry",
    TRIM(homepage) AS "Website",
    TRIM(stadt) AS "BillingCity",
    TRIM(land_region) AS "BillingCountry",
    TRIM(kundennummer) AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }}

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.

- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


# Approved mapping plan
Overview: 
Primary Key Generation: All target "Id" columns are directly mapped from their respective source table's primary key-like identifier (e.g., `kundennummer` for Account, `kontakt_id` for Contact). This ensures unique and stable identifiers.

Foreign Key Resolution: Foreign key columns (e.g., "AccountId", "Project__c") will use the exact same source column and transformation logic as the primary key of the referenced target table to ensure referential integrity. For instance, "Contact.AccountId" maps from `master_kontakte.kd_nummer`, which references `master_kunden.kundennummer` (used for "Account.Id").

Enum Mapping Strategy: All enum mappings will be case-insensitive. Source values not found in the target enum domain will result in a NULL target value, unless the target column is NOT NULL, in which case a sensible default will be applied (e.g., "Prospecting" for "Opportunity.StageName").

Date Transformation: Source date fields (`garantieende`, `zieldatum`, `go_live_datum`) are expected in various text formats. They will be parsed and converted to ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text format. If parsing fails or the source value is empty/NULL, the target will be NULL, except for "Opportunity.CloseDate" which is NOT NULL. For "Opportunity.CloseDate", if the source `zieldatum` is unparseable or NULL, it will default to '1900-01-01'.

Amount and Currency Transformation: "Amount" columns (`auftragswert`) will undergo numeric conversion, stripping non-numeric characters (currency symbols, group separators) and handling locale-specific decimal separators (e.g., ',' as decimal). NULL if conversion fails. "CurrencyIsoCode" (`waehrungscode`) will be directly mapped and normalized to uppercase.

Default Values: "IsDeleted" will default to `0` (false). "CreatedDate" and "LastModifiedDate" will be NULL as no direct source mapping is available, implying system-generated values upon ingestion. Target "Name" columns marked NOT NULL, if derived from potentially NULL source columns, will use COALESCE with their respective "Id" column to ensure the NOT NULL constraint is met.

Text Normalization: All source text fields will be `TRIM()`med before mapping. Specific case normalizations (e.g., `LOWER()`, `UPPER()`, `INITCAP()`) will be applied for enums or display names as specified per column.

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    TRIM(mp.projekt_kennung) AS "Id",
    COALESCE(TRIM(mp.projektname), TRIM(mp.projekt_kennung)) AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mp.projektstatus)) = 'aktiv' THEN 'Active'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mp.projektstatus)) = 'abgeschlossen' THEN 'Completed'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mp.projektstatus)) = 'in planung' THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mp.projektstatus)) = 'auf eis' THEN 'On Hold'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mp.projektstatus)) = 'storniert' THEN 'Cancelled'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    TO_CHAR(
        CASE
            WHEN TRIM(mp.go_live_datum) ~ '^''
            [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}'''
            $' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(mp.go_live_datum), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN TRIM(mp.go_live_datum) ~ '^''
            [0-9]{2}\.[0-9]{2}\.[0-9]{4}'''
            $' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(mp.go_live_datum), 'DD.MM.YYYY')
            WHEN TRIM(mp.go_live_datum) ~ '^''
            [0-9]{1,2}\/[0-9]{1,2}\/[0-9]{4}'''
            $' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(mp.go_live_datum), 'MM/DD/YYYY')
            WHEN TRIM(mp.go_live_datum) ~ '^''
            [0-9]{8}'''
            $' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(mp.go_live_datum), 'YYYYMMDD')
            ELSE NULL
        END,
        'YYYY-MM-DD'
    ) AS "Go_Live_Date__c",
    TRIM(mp.kunden_kennung) AS "Account__c",
    TRIM(mp.opp_kennung_ref) AS "Opportunity__c",
    TRIM(mp.projekt_kennung) AS "Legacy_Project_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_projekte') }} AS mp', 'target_table': 'Project__c', 'reasoning': 'This model transforms the `master_projekte` source table into the `Project__c` target schema. - Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.

Defaults to 'Prospecting' if no match is found, ensuring the NOT NULL constraint."}, {'target_column': 'CloseDate', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.zieldatum'], 'explanation': "Parses the close date from various text formats (MM/DD/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, YYYYMMDD, DD.MM.YYYY) into 'YYYY-MM-DD' string format. Defaults to '1900-01-01' if the source value is NULL or unparseable, satisfying the NOT NULL constraint."}, {'explanation': 'Converts the opportunity value from text to double precision, handling currency symbols and locale-specific decimal separators (comma to dot). Returns NULL if the source value is empty or cannot be converted.', 'target_column': 'Amount', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.auftragswert']}, {'target_column': 'CurrencyIsoCode', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.waehrungscode'], 'explanation': 'Maps the currency code directly, trimmed and converted to uppercase.'}, {'explanation': 'Mapped from the customer reference, trimmed for consistency with Account.Id.', 'target_column': 'AccountId', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.kunden_ref']}, {'explanation': 'Mapped directly from the opportunity identifier, trimmed for consistency.', 'target_column': 'Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.opp_kennung']}, {'explanation': 'Set to NULL as no direct source mapping is available, implying it will be system-generated.', 'target_column': 'CreatedDate', 'source_columns': []}, {'target_column': 'LastModifiedDate', 'source_columns': [], 'explanation': 'Set to NULL as no direct source mapping is available, implying it will be system-generated.'}, {'explanation': 'Set to 0 (false) as a default value.', 'target_column': 'IsDeleted', 'source_columns': []}], 'dbt_sql': '
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    TRIM(mo.opp_kennung) AS "Id",
    COALESCE(TRIM(mo.titel), TRIM(mo.opp_kennung)) AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE 'Prospecting'
    END AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(
        CASE
            WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'YYYY-MM-DD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ELSE NULL
        END,
        '1900-01-01'
    ) AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(mo.auftragswert) IS NULL OR TRIM(mo.auftragswert) = '' THEN NULL
        ELSE CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(mo.auftragswert), '[^0-9,]+', '', 'g'), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(mo.waehrungscode)) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    TRIM(mo.kunden_ref) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(mo.opp_kennung) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_opportunities') }} AS mo
LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }} AS mk
ON
    TRIM(mo.kunden_ref) = TRIM(mk.kundennummer)
'}, tool_call_id='pyd_ai_638cc131215347a69d99fbc31bf19353', provider_name='google-cloud', provider_details={'thought_signature': ''}), ToolCallPart(tool_name='final_result', args={'reasoning': 'This model transforms raw opportunity data into the target Opportunity schema. It uses direct mapping for IDs, handles date parsing for multiple formats with a default, and converts amount text to numeric, ensuring all NOT NULL constraints are met with appropriate fallbacks or defaults. The join to master_kunden ensures consistency for AccountId mapping, though no other data is pulled from it directly for this table.', 'dbt_sql': '...', 'target_table': 'Opportunity', 'column_notes': [{'explanation': 'Mapped directly from the opportunity identifier, trimmed for consistency.', 'target_column': 'Id', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.opp_kennung']}]}, tool_call_id='pyd_ai_2fc05cdb928b4252965d86da0c2cfd38')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=4794, cache_read_tokens=2664, output_tokens=2660, details={'cached_content_tokens': 2664, 'thoughts_tokens': 1192, 'text_prompt_tokens': 4794, 'text_cache_tokens': 2664, 'text_candidates_tokens': 1468}), model_name='gemini-2.5-flash', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 37, 33, 955676, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='google-cloud', provider_url='https://aiplatform.googleapis.com/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'STOP', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 37, 21, 219358, tzinfo=TzInfo(0)), 'traffic_type': 'ON_DEMAND'}, provider_response_id='wU1Bat6xDeWKhcIPhpSLmAM', finish_reason='stop')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 37, 13, 722230, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='Target table: `Installed_Asset__c`.

# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_src.master_assets  (900 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kontakte  (497 rows)

Columns:
- `kontakt_id` text NOT NULL
- `rufname` text NULL
- `familienname` text NULL
- `kontakt_email` text NULL
- `tel` text NULL
- `berufsbezeichnung` text NULL
- `rolle` text NULL
- `korrespondenzsprache` text NULL
- `kd_nummer` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kunden  (210 rows)

Columns:
- `kundennummer` text NOT NULL
- `unternehmensname` text NULL
- `erp_nr` text NULL
- `kundenklasse` text NULL
- `vertriebsgebiet` text NULL
- `industrie` text NULL
- `homepage` text NULL
- `stadt` text NULL
- `land_region` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_opportunities  (625 rows)

Columns:
- `opp_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `titel` text NULL
- `vertriebsphase` text NULL
- `zieldatum` text NULL
- `auftragswert` text NULL
- `waehrungscode` text NULL
- `kunden_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_projekte  (342 rows)

Columns:
- `projekt_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `projektname` text NULL
- `projektstatus` text NULL
- `go_live_datum` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `opp_kennung_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


# Approved mapping plan
Overview: 
Primary Key Generation: All target "Id" columns are directly mapped from their respective source table's primary key-like identifier (e.g., `kundennummer` for Account, `kontakt_id` for Contact). This ensures unique and stable identifiers.

Foreign Key Resolution: Foreign key columns (e.g., "AccountId", "Project__c") will use the exact same source column and transformation logic as the primary key of the referenced target table to ensure referential integrity. For instance, "Contact.AccountId" maps from `master_kontakte.kd_nummer`, which references `master_kunden.kundennummer` (used for "Account.Id").

Enum Mapping Strategy: All enum mappings will be case-insensitive. Source values not found in the target enum domain will result in a NULL target value, unless the target column is NOT NULL, in which case a sensible default will be applied (e.g., "Prospecting" for "Opportunity.StageName").

Date Transformation: Source date fields (`garantieende`, `zieldatum`, `go_live_datum`) are expected in various text formats. They will be parsed and converted to ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text format. If parsing fails or the source value is empty/NULL, the target will be NULL, except for "Opportunity.CloseDate" which is NOT NULL. For "Opportunity.CloseDate", if the source `zieldatum` is unparseable or NULL, it will default to '1900-01-01'.

Amount and Currency Transformation: "Amount" columns (`auftragswert`) will undergo numeric conversion, stripping non-numeric characters (currency symbols, group separators) and handling locale-specific decimal separators (e.g., ',' as decimal). NULL if conversion fails. "CurrencyIsoCode" (`waehrungscode`) will be directly mapped and normalized to uppercase.

Default Values: "IsDeleted" will default to `0` (false). "CreatedDate" and "LastModifiedDate" will be NULL as no direct source mapping is available, implying system-generated values upon ingestion. Target "Name" columns marked NOT NULL, if derived from potentially NULL source columns, will use COALESCE with their respective "Id" column to ensure the NOT NULL constraint is met.

Text Normalization: All source text fields will be `TRIM()`med before mapping. Specific case normalizations (e.g., `LOWER()`, `UPPER()`, `INITCAP()`) will be applied for enums or display names as specified per column.
Source tables: fixture_master_src.master_assets, fixture_master_src.master_kunden, fixture_master_src.master_projekte
Join keys: master_assets.kunden_kennung = master_kunden.kundennummer, master_assets.projekt_kennung = master_projekte.projekt_kennung
- Id <- asset_kennung (TRIM(source.asset_kennung))
- Name <- asset_name, asset_kennung (COALESCE(TRIM(source.asset_name), TRIM(source.asset_kennung)) (target NOT NULL))
- Serial_Number__c <- serien_nummer (TRIM(source.serien_nummer))
- Warranty_End_Date__c <- garantieende (Parse text (source.garantieende) to ISO YYYY-MM-DD. NULL if NULL or unparseable.)
- Account__c <- kunden_kennung (TRIM(source.kunden_kennung) (consistent with Account.Id))
- Project__c <- projekt_kennung (TRIM(source.projekt_kennung) (consistent with Project__c.Id))
- Legacy_Asset_ID__c <- asset_kennung (TRIM(source.asset_kennung))
- CreatedDate <- (unspecified) (NULL (system-generated))
- LastModifiedDate <- (unspecified) (NULL (system-generated))
- IsDeleted <- (unspecified) (0)

Follow the plan. ModelResponse(parts=[ToolCallPart(tool_name='final_result', args={'reasoning': 'The model directly maps asset information from fixture_master_src.master_assets. It ensures unique identifiers, handles date parsing for warranty end dates across various formats, and establishes foreign keys to Account and Project__c using consistent transformation logic for linked IDs. Defaults are applied for system-generated fields and IsDeleted.', 'dbt_sql': '
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    TRIM(ma.asset_kennung) AS "Id",
    COALESCE(TRIM(ma.asset_name), TRIM(ma.asset_kennung)) AS "Name",
    TRIM(ma.serien_nummer) AS "Serial_Number__c",
    CASE
        WHEN ma.garantieende ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' AND ma.garantieende <> '0000-00-00' THEN ma.garantieende

- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 37, 46, 527454, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='Your previous dbt model for `Opportunity` failed.

Previous SQL:
```sql

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    TRIM(mo.opp_kennung) AS "Id",
    COALESCE(TRIM(mo.titel), TRIM(mo.opp_kennung)) AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE 'Prospecting'
    END AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(
        CASE
            WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'YYYY-MM-DD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ELSE NULL
        END,
        '1900-01-01'
    ) AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(mo.auftragswert) IS NULL OR TRIM(mo.auftragswert) = '' THEN NULL
        ELSE CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(mo.auftragswert), '[^0-9,]+', '', 'g'), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(mo.waehrungscode)) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    TRIM(mo.kunden_ref) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(mo.opp_kennung) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_opportunities') }} AS mo
LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }} AS mk
ON
    TRIM(mo.kunden_ref) = TRIM(mk.kundennummer)

```

        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE 'Prospecting'
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'YYYY-MM-DD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')

        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mo.vertriebsphase)) = 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE 'Prospecting'
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'YYYY-MM-DD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(mo.auftragswert) IS NULL OR TRIM(mo.auftragswert) = '' THEN NULL
        ELSE
            -- Clean the value, then check if it is an empty string after cleaning before casting.
            CASE
                WHEN REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(mo.auftragswert), '[^0-9,]+', '', 'g'), ',', '.') = '' THEN NULL
                ELSE CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(mo.auftragswert), '[^0-9,]+', '', 'g'), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
            END
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(mo.waehrungscode)) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    TRIM(mo.kunden_ref) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(mo.opp_kennung) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_opportunities') }} AS mo


ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 38, 25, 128996, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='Your previous dbt model for `Project__c` failed.

```sql

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    TRIM(mp.projekt_kennung) AS "Id",
    COALESCE(TRIM(mp.projektname), TRIM(mp.projekt_kennung)) AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mp.projektstatus)) = 'aktiv' THEN 'Active'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mp.projektstatus)) = 'abgeschlossen' THEN 'Completed'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mp.projektstatus)) = 'in planung' THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mp.projektstatus)) = 'auf eis' THEN 'On Hold'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mp.projektstatus)) = 'storniert' THEN 'Cancelled'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    TO_CHAR(
        CASE
            WHEN TRIM(mp.go_live_datum) ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(mp.go_live_datum), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN TRIM(mp.go_live_datum) ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(mp.go_live_datum), 'DD.MM.YYYY')
            WHEN TRIM(mp.go_live_datum) ~ '^\d{1,2}\/\d{1,2}\/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(mp.go_live_datum), 'MM/DD/YYYY')
            WHEN TRIM(mp.go_live_datum) ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(mp.go_live_datum), 'YYYYMMDD')
            ELSE NULL
        END,
        'YYYY-MM-DD'
    ) AS "Go_Live_Date__c",
    TRIM(mp.kunden_kennung) AS "Account__c",
    TRIM(mp.opp_kennung_ref) AS "Opportunity__c",
    TRIM(mp.projekt_kennung) AS "Legacy_Project_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_projekte') }} AS mp

```

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 38, 25, 129049, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='Your previous dbt model for `Contact` failed.

Previous SQL:
```sql

-- dbt model for Contact

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    TRIM(kontakte.kontakt_id) AS "Id",
    TRIM(kontakte.rufname) AS "FirstName",
    COALESCE(TRIM(kontakte.familienname), 'Unknown') AS "LastName",
    LOWER(TRIM(kontakte.kontakt_email)) AS "Email",
    TRIM(kontakte.tel) AS "Phone",
    TRIM(kontakte.berufsbezeichnung) AS "Title",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kontakte.rolle)) = 'decision maker' THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kontakte.rolle)) = 'end user' THEN 'End User'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kontakte.rolle)) = 'technical contact' THEN 'Technical Contact'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kontakte.rolle)) = 'executive sponsor' THEN 'Executive Sponsor'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    CASE UPPER(TRIM(kontakte.korrespondenzsprache))
        WHEN 'DE' THEN 'DE'
        WHEN 'EN' THEN 'EN'
        WHEN 'FR' THEN 'FR'
        WHEN 'ES' THEN 'ES'
        WHEN 'IT' THEN 'IT'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Preferred_Language__c",
    TRIM(kontakte.kd_nummer) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(kontakte.kontakt_id) AS "Legacy_Contact_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kontakte') }} AS kontakte

```

dbt error:
Skipping due to fail_fast

When fixing errors:

- Ensure every mixed-case output column uses a double-quoted alias: `expr AS "ColumnName"`.

- Ensure every CAST includes `AS <type>`: `CAST(expr AS DOUBLE PRECISION)`, not `CAST(expr)`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

# Source database

`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.

- Ensure every CAST includes `AS <type>`: `CAST(expr AS DOUBLE PRECISION)`, not `CAST(expr)`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.

ModelResponse(parts=[ToolCallPart(tool_name='final_result', args={'reasoning': 'This model transforms raw opportunity data into the target schema, focusing on robust parsing for varying date and amount formats, and mapping source sales stages and currency codes to their target enum and ISO equivalents. All NOT NULL constraints are satisfied with appropriate fallbacks or default values.', 'target_table': 'Opportunity', 'column_notes': [{'explanation': 'Directly mapped from the source opportunity identifier.', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.opp_kennung'], 'target_column': 'Id'}, {'target_column': 'Name', 'explanation': 'Mapped from the opportunity title, falling back to the opportunity identifier if the title is null to ensure a NOT NULL value.', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.titel', 'master_opportunities.opp_kennung']}, {'target_column': 'StageName', 'explanation': "Translated from source sales phase into the target enum values, with unmapped values defaulting to 'Prospecting' to satisfy the NOT NULL constraint.", 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.vertriebsphase']}, {'target_column': 'CloseDate', 'explanation': 'Parsed from various source date formats (YYYY-MM-DD, DD.MM.YYYY, M/D/YYYY, YYYYMMDD) and formatted as YYYY-MM-DD TEXT. Defaults to CURRENT_DATE if parsing fails or the source is NULL to satisfy the NOT NULL constraint.', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.zieldatum']}, {'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.auftragswert'], 'explanation': 'Cleaned by removing non-numeric characters and handling locale-specific decimal separators (comma for European, dot for American) before casting to DOUBLE PRECISION.', 'target_column': 'Amount'}, {'target_column': 'CurrencyIsoCode', 'explanation': "Mapped from source currency names/symbols to standard ISO currency codes (e.g., 'Dollar' to 'USD', '€' to 'EUR').", 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.waehrungscode']}, {'explanation': "Joined with 'master_kunden' on 'kunden_ref' to retrieve the customer's identifier, which serves as the AccountId.", 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.kunden_ref', 'master_kunden.kundennummer'], 'target_column': 'AccountId'}, {'explanation': 'Directly mapped from the source opportunity identifier for legacy tracking.', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.opp_kennung'], 'target_column': 'Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c'}, {'target_column': 'CreatedDate', 'explanation': 'No direct source, defaulted to NULL as TEXT.', 'source_columns': []}, {'target_column': 'LastModifiedDate', 'source_columns': [], 'explanation': 'No direct source, defaulted to NULL as TEXT.'}, {'target_column': 'IsDeleted', 'explanation': 'Defaulted to 0 (integer) as no source column is available.', 'source_columns': []}], 'dbt_sql': '
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    so.opp_kennung AS "Id",
    COALESCE(so.titel, so.opp_kennung) AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(so.vertriebsphase)) IN ('closed won', 'won', 'gewonnen', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)', 'closed won') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(so.vertriebsphase)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)', 'lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(so.vertriebsphase)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect', 'in kontakt') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(so.vertriebsphase)) IN ('qualification', 'qualifikation', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(so.vertriebsphase)) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        ELSE 'Prospecting' -- Default stage for unmapped or null values
    END AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(
        CASE
            WHEN so.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN CAST(so.zieldatum AS DATE)
            WHEN so.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(so.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')
            WHEN so.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(so.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY')
            WHEN so.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(so.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')
            ELSE NULL
        END,
        CURRENT_DATE
    )::TEXT AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN so.auftragswert IS NULL THEN NULL
        ELSE
            CAST(TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(
                CASE
                    -- If the string contains a comma, assume European format (dot for thousands, comma for decimal)
                    WHEN so.auftragswert LIKE '%,' || REPEAT('_%_', 0) || '%' THEN REPLACE(REPLACE(so.auftragswert, '.', ''), ',', '.')
                    -- Otherwise, assume American format (comma for thousands, dot for decimal)
                    ELSE REPLACE(so.auftragswert, ',', '')
                END,
            '[^-0-9.]', '', 'g')) AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN so.waehrungscode = 'Dollar' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN so.waehrungscode = '€' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN so.waehrungscode = 'GBP' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN so.waehrungscode = 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    fk_customer.kundennummer AS "AccountId",
    so.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_opportunities') }} AS so
LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }} AS fk_customer
    ON so.kunden_ref = fk_customer.kundennummer;
'}, tool_call_id='pyd_ai_242d62ce026c4157badc42663dafed27', provider_name='google-cloud', provider_details={'thought_signature': ''})], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=5097, cache_read_tokens=4340, output_tokens=6150, details={'cached_content_tokens': 4340, 'thoughts_tokens': 4837, 'text_prompt_tokens': 5097, 'text_cache_tokens': 4340, 'text_candidates_tokens': 1313}), model_name='gemini-2.5-flash', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 40, 36, 842826, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='google-cloud', provider_url='https://aiplatform.googleapis.com/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'STOP', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 40, 4, 720483, tzinfo=TzInfo(0)), 'traffic_type': 'ON_DEMAND'}, provider_response_id='ZE5BauP8K-m0trIP6vnLiAE', finish_reason='stop')

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.

- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).

- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.

- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

```sql
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(so.vertriebsphase)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)', 'lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(so.vertriebsphase)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect', 'in kontakt') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(so.vertriebsphase)) IN ('qualification', 'qualifikation', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(so.vertriebsphase)) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        ELSE 'Prospecting' -- Default stage for unmapped or null values
    END AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(
        CASE
            WHEN so.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN CAST(so.zieldatum AS DATE)
            WHEN so.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(so.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')
            WHEN so.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(so.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY')
            WHEN so.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(so.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')
            ELSE NULL
        END,
        CURRENT_DATE
    )::TEXT AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN so.auftragswert IS NULL THEN NULL
        ELSE
            CAST(TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(
                CASE
                    -- If the string contains a comma, assume European format (dot for thousands, comma for decimal)
                    WHEN so.auftragswert LIKE '%,' || REPEAT('_%_', 0) || '%' THEN REPLACE(REPLACE(so.auftragswert, '.', ''), ',', '.')
                    -- Otherwise, assume American format (comma for thousands, dot for decimal)
                    ELSE REPLACE(so.auftragswert, ',', '')
                END,
            '[^-0-9.]', '', 'g')) AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN so.waehrungscode = 'Dollar' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN so.waehrungscode = '€' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN so.waehrungscode = 'GBP' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN so.waehrungscode = 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    fk_customer.kundennummer AS "AccountId",
    so.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_opportunities') }} AS so
LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }} AS fk_customer
    ON so.kunden_ref = fk_customer.kundennummer;

```

        ELSE NULL
